channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gamedeveloper.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gamedeveloper.com

item.title: MindsEye developer announces more job cuts while alleging 'corporate sabotage'
item.content:encoded:
Chris Kerr,

Senior Editor, News,GameDeveloper.com

March 5, 2026

2 Min Read

Image via Build A Rocket Boy

MindsEye developer Build A Rocket Boy (BARB), the studio established by former Rockstar North president Leslie Benzies, has announced its second round of job cuts in eight months.

In a statement posted on LinkedIn by BARB chief exec Mark Gerhard, the UK studio confirmed it has laid off an undisclosed number of employees. 

Notably, it also suggested the rocky launch of MindsEye, which was critically panned and preceded by a number of key executive departures at BARB, was the result of "organised espionage and corporate sabotage." 

"As leaders we take responsibility for the outcomes of our projects and the decisions act follow. At the same time the launch period was affected by factors beyond normal operational challenges and a competitive environment," wrote Gerhard. 

"Over the past months we have been working with external partners and legal advisors to investigate the criminal activity that took place around our launch. That work has taken far longer than expected, but it has now resulted in overwhelming evidence of organised espionage and corporate sabotage affecting MindsEye. Because this matter is moving towards prosecution, we cannot share the full details publicly yet."

Related:Jackbox Games jumps feet-first into third-party publishing biz

Gerhard said the development team has "worked relentlessly" to support MindsEye while the investigation progressed but claimed the prolonged impact of a difficult launch has resulted in the need for more layoffs.

BARB initially made a number of employees redundant in June 2025. Although it didn't confirm how many roles were being eliminated, IGN indicated the process could result in over 100 layoffs. 

Months later, around 100 current and former BARB employees signed an open letter containing allegations of "disastrous" mismanagement. Those signatories claimed studio leadership pushed employees to work "unbearable levels of overtime" while completely mishandling the layoff process. 

In February this year, BARB was accused of installing surveillance technology on work machines without the knowledge of employees.

Game Developer has reached out to BARB for comment.

About the Author

Senior Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Game Developer news editor Chris Kerr is an award-winning reporter with over a decade of experience in the game industry. His byline has appeared in notable print and digital publications including Edge, Stuff, Wireframe, International Business Times, and PocketGamer.biz. Throughout his career, Chris has covered major industry events including GDC, PAX Australia, Gamescom, Paris Games Week, and Develop Brighton. 



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/mindseye-developer-announces-more-job-cuts-while-alleging-corporate-sabotage-
